name: Frankish Kingdom / Carolingian Empire
note: Clovis I unified the Franks (486) and converted to Catholic Christianity, giving the Franks papal legitimacy. Charlemagne (768–814) conquered the Saxons, Lombards, and Avars; crowned Emperor by Pope Leo III on Christmas 800. The first attempt at a unified Western European empire since Rome. Treaty of Verdun (843) split the empire into West Francia (future France), East Francia (future Germany), and Middle Francia.

figures.name: Clovis I
figures.significance: United the Frankish tribes; converted to Catholic Christianity 496 — the act that aligned the Franks with the papacy against the Arian Visigoths and Burgundians; baptism at Reims is the symbolic founding of Christian France

figures.name: Charlemagne (Karl der Große)
figures.significance: The greatest medieval European ruler; unified most of western Europe; crowned Emperor 800; the 'Father of Europe'. Both France and Germany claim him as their founding king.

figures.name: Louis the Pious
figures.significance: Charlemagne's heir; pious but ineffective at holding the empire together against his sons' civil wars; his death triggered the Treaty of Verdun partition
